proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary feature among feature_005, feature_006, feature_008, feature_011, feature_013, feature_015, feature_017, feature_019, feature_021, feature_023, feature_027 is associated with the binary outcome objective_response (chi-square test).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 (35% vs 15% response, diff=-19.2pp, p≈0), feature_008 (16% vs 36%, diff=+20.2pp, p≈0), feature_015 (26% vs 16%, diff=-10.1pp, p=2e-98), feature_021 (25% vs 15%, diff=-10.1pp, p=5e-56), feature_027 (24% vs 18%, diff=-6.6pp, p=3e-9) all highly significant. feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004 show no signal (p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal variable feature_001 (range 0-2) are associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher levels of the ordinal variable feature_010 (range 6-10) are associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_001']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 shows a strong monotonic decrease in response: level 0 → 28.2%, level 1 → 22.8%, level 2 → 18.2%. Logistic coef = -0.285 per level (p=9.5e-73). Likely an ordinal performance-status / stage variable.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_010']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_010 (Gleason-like, 6-10) shows essentially flat response across levels (24.4%, 23.7%, 24.2%, 24.2%, 24.2%); coef=0.005, p=0.61. No prognostic signal alone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 (highly skewed continuous lab; likely PSA-like) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_002 (continuous, mean ~3.8; likely albumin-like) is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher feature_020 (count-like, mean ~3.8) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_024 (continuous, mean ~6) is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age (feature_016, mean 65) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_022']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 strongly negatively associated with response: mean 47.2 in non-responders vs 28.2 in responders, logit coef = -0.0047 per unit, p=1.6e-94.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_002']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_002 modestly positive: 3.80 vs 3.82, coef=+0.096, p=4.4e-6. Weak but significant.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_020']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_020 negatively associated: coef=-0.032 per unit, p=3.4e-29.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_024']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 negatively associated: coef=-0.007, p=5.6e-8.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_016']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 (age-like, range 30-90) shows no association: mean 64.98 vs 65.01, coef=+0.0002, p=0.82.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all 31 informative features, feature_008 has the largest positive adjusted effect and feature_013 has the largest negative adjusted effect among binary variables.

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[all_features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pseudo R^2 = 0.124. Strongest adjusted effects (in descending |z|): feature_008 +1.20 (p≈0), feature_013 -1.13 (p≈0), feature_015 -0.69 (p=1e-108), feature_021 -0.74 (p=5e-64), feature_001 -0.33/level (p=1e-82), feature_020 -0.036 (p=2e-33), feature_027 -0.43 (p=3e-9), feature_002 +0.13 (p=2e-8), feature_022 -0.001 (p=3e-8), feature_024 -0.007 (p=9e-8). All other features non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (likely PSA) is much higher in patients with feature_013=1 than feature_013=0, suggesting feature_013 indexes high tumor burden / advanced disease.

The five candidate response-modifying binary variables (feature_008, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) are statistically independent of each other and of feature_001 (suggesting random/orthogonal assignment for the candidate treatment indicator).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_013')['feature_022'].describe()
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 median 7.38 (IQR 3.77-14.45) when feature_013=0 vs median 32.94 (IQR 14.62-73.85) when feature_013=1. Strong correlation: in highest feature_022 quintile, 95% have feature_013=1; in lowest quintile, only 17.5%.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency for all pairs and crosstab vs feature_001
analyses.result_summary: All 10 pairwise chi-square p-values among feature_008/013/015/021/027 are >0.05 except feature_008 vs feature_027 (p=0.071). Cross-tabs vs feature_001 show identical proportions across feature_001 levels for each (e.g., feature_008=1 frequencies 40.1%/40.3%/39.6% across feature_001=0/1/2). Strong evidence of independent/random assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is much larger when feature_013=0 than when feature_013=1 (positive interaction in the protective direction; treatment effect concentrated in feature_013=0 patients).

analyses.code: Logit(y, add_constant(df[['feature_008','feature_013','feature_008*feature_013']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates: feature_013=0, feature_008=0 → 16.9%; feature_013=0, feature_008=1 → 60.96%; feature_013=1, feature_008=0 → 15.0%; feature_013=1, feature_008=1 → 15.8%. The treatment effect is +44.0 pp when feature_013=0 vs +0.8 pp when feature_013=1. Interaction term coef = -1.978, p≈0 (z = -42.7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 each negatively interact with feature_008, while feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 positively interact with each other (a sign indicating mutual partial 'redundancy' once one is present).

analyses.code: for each binary pair, fit Logit(y ~ a + b + a*b)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients with feature_008: × feature_013 = -1.98 (p≈0), × feature_015 = -1.24 (p=1e-93), × feature_021 = -1.14 (p=3e-42), × feature_027 = -1.28 (p=2e-19) — all strongly negative. Pairwise interactions among the four blockers are positive (e.g., feature_013×feature_015 = +1.14, p=1e-81), consistent with each independently being able to abolish the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with feature_013=0 and feature_008=1 (treated low-burden), feature_015=1, feature_021=1, and feature_027=1 each substantially reduce objective_response.

analyses.code: stratified means within feature_013=0 & feature_008=1
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0 & feature_008=1 (n=9,039, overall 60.96% response): feature_015=0 → 71.7% / feature_015=1 → 17.7% (diff -54.0 pp); feature_021=0 → 66.1% / feature_021=1 → 14.5% (diff -51.7 pp); feature_027=0 → 62.2% / feature_027=1 → 19.2% (diff -43.0 pp). Each blocker strongly suppresses response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_013=1 (high burden) stratum, feature_008 confers no benefit and the only meaningful prognostic predictor of objective_response is feature_001 (declining response with higher level).

analyses.code: stratified means within feature_013=1
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=1 (n=27,481, overall 15.4% response): feature_008 effect +0.8 pp; feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 each null (|diff|<1 pp). Only feature_001 still tracks response: level 0 → 19.0%, level 1 → 14.5%, level 2 → 9.8%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the four-way subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0, feature_008=1 yields a much higher response rate than feature_008=0 (>=40 pp absolute increase). Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is essentially null.

analyses.code: Two-way comparison stratified by joint blocker condition
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (n=15,681; 31.4% of cohort): feature_008=0 → 17.21%, feature_008=1 → 79.78% (diff +62.57 pp, chi-square p≈0). Outside subgroup (n=34,319): feature_008=0 → 15.30%, feature_008=1 → 16.06% (diff +0.76 pp, NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An automatically-induced regression tree on the estimated individual treatment effect of feature_008 will recover the same four-feature blocker definition (feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) as the dominant modifiers, with no additional important splits.

analyses.code: Two-model T-learner (logistic) ITE = P(Y|T=1,X)-P(Y|T=0,X) → DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4)
analyses.result_summary: Tree splits in order: feature_013, then feature_015, then feature_021, then feature_027. Feature importances: feature_013 0.728, feature_015 0.182, feature_021 0.082, feature_027 0.006; all other features <0.005. Confirms the four-blocker subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (feature_013=feature_015=feature_021=feature_027=0), no continuous biomarker (feature_022, feature_002, feature_020, feature_024, feature_016, feature_026, feature_009, feature_029, feature_003, feature_012, feature_025, feature_028, feature_007, feature_014, feature_032, feature_018, feature_031) shows a strong interaction with feature_008.

analyses.code: for each c: Logit(y ~ feature_008 + c + feature_008*c) within favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: All continuous-feature × feature_008 interaction p-values within the favorable subgroup are >0.05; smallest is feature_026 (p=0.058) and feature_001 (p=0.069), both with small effect sizes (≤0.11 logit). feature_008's effect is uniform across continuous biomarker values within this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In control patients (feature_008=0), feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 have negligible effect on objective_response — they are predictive (treatment-effect modifiers) rather than prognostic.

analyses.code: stratified means within feature_008=0 across each blocker
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_008=0 (n=29,924): feature_013 0/1 → 16.93%/15.05% (diff -1.88 pp); feature_015 0/1 → 15.95%/15.65% (diff -0.30 pp); feature_021 0/1 → 16.02%/14.71% (diff -1.31 pp); feature_027 0/1 → 15.81%/18.44% (diff +2.63 pp). All differences small. Within feature_008=1 these same features carve out 60+ pp gaps (e.g., feature_013 60.96% vs 15.81%). Confirms predictive (effect-modifying) rather than prognostic role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A single logistic model with feature_008 main effect and feature_008 × {feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, feature_001} interactions yields a large positive feature_008 main effect and large negative interactions for feature_013/015/021/027 but not for feature_001.

analyses.code: Logit on feature_008, blockers, feature_001 + their interactions with feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_008 = +2.57 (p≈0). Interactions: × feature_013 = -2.22 (p≈0), × feature_015 = -1.63 (p=2e-140), × feature_021 = -1.55 (p=4e-67), × feature_027 = -1.66 (p=2e-27), × feature_001 = +0.045 (p=0.21, NS). feature_001 acts as prognostic main effect (-0.38/level) but does not modify treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 declines monotonically with the count of blockers (feature_013+feature_015+feature_021+feature_027) and is essentially zero when at least one blocker is present.

analyses.code: compute n_blockers, stratify response by n_blockers and feature_008
analyses.result_summary: n_blockers=0: feature_008 effect +62.6 pp (17.2%→79.8%, n=15,681). n_blockers=1: +0.9 pp (15.4%→16.3%, n=25,372). n_blockers=2: +0.8 pp (14.6%→15.3%, n=8,186). n_blockers≥3: ~0 to slightly negative (n=761). The 'all blockers absent' condition is necessary and sufficient for benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Three-way interactions feature_008 × blocker_a × blocker_b are positive (sub-additive), reflecting the saturating structure where any one blocker fully cancels the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Logit on feature_008, a, b, feature_008*a, feature_008*b, feature_008*a*b for each blocker pair
analyses.result_summary: All triple-interactions feature_008 × blocker_i × blocker_j are large and positive: feature_013×feature_015=+2.38 (p=1e-83), feature_013×feature_021=+2.47 (p=5e-48), feature_013×feature_027=+1.87 (p=6e-11), feature_015×feature_021=+1.02 (p=1e-6), feature_015×feature_027=+1.19 (p=6e-4); only feature_021×feature_027 NS (p=0.33, sparse). Confirms saturating any-blocker structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_013=0 patients, the continuous biomarker feature_022 (PSA-like) does NOT further modify the feature_008 treatment effect once feature_013 is conditioned on.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_008 + log(feature_022) + interaction) within feature_013=0
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0 (n=22,519), the log(feature_022) × feature_008 interaction is -0.012 (p=0.72). feature_013 captures the binarized treatment-effect-modifier information; residual feature_022 has only a small main prognostic effect (-0.062, p=0.009).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ALL binary features, only feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 are large-effect modifiers of feature_008 (interaction |coef|>1 and p<1e-15); other binaries (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004) show no meaningful interaction.

analyses.code: for each binary f: Logit(y ~ feature_008 + f + feature_008*f)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction effects with feature_008: feature_013 -1.98 p≈0; feature_015 -1.24 p=1e-93; feature_027 -1.28 p=2e-19; feature_021 -1.14 p=3e-42; feature_011 -0.106 p=0.023 (tiny); feature_017 -0.111 p=0.118; feature_005 +0.037 p=0.40; feature_006 -0.009 p=0.86; feature_023 -0.007 p=0.89; feature_004 -0.006 p=0.95; feature_019 0.000 p=1.00. Confirms exactly four large modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among untreated patients (feature_008=0), the strongest continuous prognostic predictors of objective_response are feature_020 (negative), feature_022 (negative), feature_024 (negative), and feature_002 (positive); other continuous features have no detectable prognostic association.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ c) within feature_008=0, for each continuous c
analyses.result_summary: Significant continuous predictors among untreated: feature_020 coef=-0.0374 p=4e-18; feature_022 coef=-0.0013 p=1e-8; feature_024 coef=-0.0092 p=5e-6; feature_002 coef=+0.148 p=3e-6. Non-significant: feature_016 (age), feature_018, feature_031, feature_026, feature_009, feature_029, feature_003, feature_012, feature_025, feature_028, feature_007, feature_014, feature_032 (all p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, feature_001 (ordinal 0/1/2) is prognostic (lower response at higher levels in BOTH treatment arms), but the absolute treatment benefit of feature_008 remains large (>=55 pp) at every feature_001 level.

analyses.code: stratified means within favorable subgroup by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, feature_008 effect by feature_001: level 0 → 21.3% vs 85.9% (+64.6 pp); level 1 → 15.9% vs 78.1% (+62.2 pp); level 2 → 11.9% vs 70.9% (+59.0 pp). Borderline modest decline (interaction p=0.07-0.10). Treatment benefit preserved at all levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, after adjusting for feature_001, feature_022, feature_020, feature_024, feature_002, and age (feature_016), the adjusted odds ratio of feature_008 for objective_response exceeds 15.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_008 + feature_001 + feature_022 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_002 + feature_016) within favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_008 coef = +3.019 (OR 20.5, 95% CI 18.8-22.4, p≈0). feature_001 -0.40/level (p=4e-38), feature_022 -0.006 (p=4e-5), feature_020 -0.045 (p=2e-16), feature_024 -0.011 (p=2e-6), feature_002 +0.168 (p=6e-5), feature_016 +0.003 (p=0.15). Treatment effect highly robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 corresponds approximately to a high-feature_022 (PSA-like) cutoff: most patients with feature_022 above the population median have feature_013=1, but it is not a deterministic threshold (so feature_013 carries information beyond a continuous PSA cut).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(qcut(df['feature_022'], 5), df['feature_013'], normalize='index')
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 share by feature_022 quintile: Q1 17.5%, Q2 33.2%, Q3 53.0%, Q4 76.3%, Q5 94.8%. Strong but imperfect concordance — feature_013 indexes high tumor burden / advanced status (e.g., metastatic CRPC) that correlates with but is not identical to high PSA.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_008 (the candidate effective treatment) is approximately independent of all other features, consistent with random assignment / balanced exposure.

analyses.code: Logit(feature_008 ~ all other features)
analyses.result_summary: In multivariable logistic, no covariate predicts feature_008 receipt at p<0.01; smallest p is feature_017 at 0.036 with coef -0.063 (tiny). Strong evidence feature_008 is randomly assigned, supporting causal interpretation of the >60 pp subgroup treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is strongly predicted by feature_022 (PSA), confirming feature_013 indexes high tumor burden; feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 have weaker or no clear continuous correlates and are most plausibly resistance-marker / alternative-therapy indicators rather than disease burden.

analyses.code: Logit(blocker ~ all other features) for each blocker
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 ~ feature_022 (coef +0.061, p≈0) — dominant. feature_015 ~ feature_022 (+0.0003, p=0.015 — weak), feature_032 (+0.045, p=0.043), and otherwise no large predictors. feature_021 best predicted by feature_007 (-0.012, p=0.012) and feature_022 (+0.0003, p=0.046). feature_027 has no significant predictors. These four 'blockers' carry information not captured by other features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: For the outcome objective_response, the treatment indicator feature_008 produces an absolute response-rate increase of approximately +63 percentage points (from ~17% to ~80%) only in patients who simultaneously satisfy feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0; in the complement subgroup the effect of feature_008 is essentially zero (≤+1 pp). Each of feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 = 1 individually abolishes the treatment effect; their joint absence is necessary AND sufficient for benefit.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC HYPOTHESIS: Independent of treatment, lower objective_response is associated with higher feature_001 (ordinal 0-2), higher feature_022 (PSA-like), higher feature_020, and higher feature_024; higher feature_002 is associated with higher response. These are prognostic main effects, not treatment-effect modifiers.

analyses.code: favorable = (df['feature_013']==0) & (df['feature_015']==0) & (df['feature_021']==0) & (df['feature_027']==0); compare feature_008 within favorable vs complement; test on full model
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (n=15,681, 31.4% of cohort): feature_008=0 → 17.21%, feature_008=1 → 79.78%, RD = +62.57 pp, RR = 4.64, OR = 19.2 (unadj), 20.5 (adjusted for feature_001, feature_022, feature_020, feature_024, feature_002, feature_016), p≈0. Complement (n=34,319): feature_008=0 → 15.30%, feature_008=1 → 16.06%, RD = +0.76 pp, p=0.07. Decision-tree CATE recovery confirms the same four-feature definition with no important additional split.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_001 + feature_022 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_002) within feature_008=0
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_008=0 (untreated, n=29,924): feature_001 -0.353/level (p=8e-46), feature_022 -0.0013 (p=1e-8), feature_020 -0.037 (p=4e-18), feature_024 -0.009 (p=5e-6), feature_002 +0.148 (p=3e-6). All four prognostic directions confirmed. None of these is a substantial feature_008-effect modifier within the favorable subgroup (all interaction p>0.05).
